title: Fix queue element access
body: Currently, Verilator in some cases incorrectly classifies access to queue element as rvalue. It throws then compilation errors, because `at` and `atBack` methods are used instead of `atWrite` and `atWriteAppendBack`. It happens e.g. when a queue or dynamic array element is passed to an output port of a task or if it is a target of dynamic cast. I decided to compute the type of access using V3LinkLValue, as it is used for variable references and possibly covers also other cases.
